questions.prompt: What does 'diminishing returns to capital' mean in the Solow model?
choices: Returns to capital eventually fall to zero everywhere
Each additional unit of capital per worker adds less output than the previous one
Capital depreciates faster as the country gets richer
Workers become less productive as capital grows
questions.explanation: Diminishing returns means the production function y = k^α is concave: the marginal product of k falls as k rises.

questions.prompt: A country permanently raises its savings rate from 25% to 50%. In the long run:
choices: Output per worker grows faster forever
Output per worker reaches a higher level but eventually stops growing
Output per worker falls because consumption is lower
questions.explanation: Higher saving moves the steady state up — a one-time level gain. Long-run growth is determined by technology, not saving.

questions.prompt: Which of the following raise the steady-state capital per worker k*?
choices: A higher population growth rate n
questions.explanation: Higher s and higher α raise k*. Higher n or higher δ raise the break-even line, lowering k*.

questions.prompt: Why did South Korea overtake Ghana between 1960 and 2025, even though both had similar GDP per person in 1960?
choices: South Korea had a higher savings rate, which alone is enough to explain the gap in the Solow model
South Korea's natural resources were larger
South Korea adopted and developed better technologies, plus invested heavily — the model points to technology as the driver of sustained growth
questions.explanation: Saving alone produces a level effect with no permanent growth in Solow. Sustained divergence requires technology adoption.

furtherReading.title: The Productivity Puzzle: AI, Technology Adoption and the Workforce
furtherReading.why: Richmond Fed investigation of why massive AI capex has not yet translated to measurable productivity growth — a live Solow-paradox case study with hard data.
